maml:description.maml:para: Adds one or more FileGroup objects to a Database object.

This command adds one or more FileGroup objects to a Database object's FileGroups collection. This is useful when you have created FileGroup objects using New-SqlDscFileGroup and want to associate them with a Database.
Specifies the Database object to which the FileGroups will be added.
Specifies one or more FileGroup objects to add to the Database. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies that the FileGroup should be added without confirmation.
Returns the FileGroup objects that were added to the Database.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the Database object to which the FileGroups will be added.
Specifies one or more FileGroup objects to add to the Database. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies that the FileGroup should be added without confirmation.
Returns the FileGroup objects that were added to the Database.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: FileGroup objects that will be added to the Database.
This cmdlet does not generate output by default.
When the PassThru parameter is specified, returns the FileGroup objects that were added.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a single FileGroup to the Database.
Adds multiple FileGroups to the Database via pipeline and returns the FileGroup objects.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds an SSL certificate binding for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Adds an SSL certificate binding for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `CreateSSLCertificateBinding` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command binds an SSL certificate to a specific application, IP address, and port for the Reporting Services instance. URL reservations must be set prior for the specified application to determine if the TLS/SSL certificate binding is valid.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application for which to create the SSL binding. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the thumbprint (hash) of the SSL certificate to bind. The certificate must be installed in the local machine certificate store.
Specifies the IP address for the SSL binding. Use '0.0.0.0' to bind to all IP addresses. Default value is '0.0.0.0'.
Specifies the port number for the SSL binding. Default value is 443.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the operation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after adding the SSL certificate binding.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after adding the SSL certificate binding.

maml:description.maml:para: Adds a URL reservation for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Adds a URL reservation for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `ReserveUrl` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command reserves a URL for a specific application in the Reporting Services instance. The application can be the Report Server Web Service, the Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+), or the Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application for which to reserve the URL. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the URL string to reserve. The URL string format is typically 'http://+:80' or 'https://+:443' where the plus sign (+) is a wildcard that matches all hostnames.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the URL reservation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after adding the URL reservation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Performs a backup of a SQL Server database.

This command performs a backup of a SQL Server database using SQL Server Management Objects (SMO). It supports full, differential, and transaction log backups with options for compression, verification, and copy-only backups.
Specifies the full path to the backup file. For full and differential backups, use the `.bak` extension. For transaction log backups, use the `.trn` extension.
Specifies the type of backup to perform. Valid values are 'Full', 'Differential', and 'Log'. Default value is 'Full'.
Specifies that checksums should be calculated and verified during the backup operation to help detect backup media errors.
Specifies that the backup should be compressed. Backup compression requires SQL Server 2008 Enterprise or later, or SQL Server 2008 R2 Standard or later.

Specifies that a copy-only backup should be performed. Copy-only backups do not affect the sequence of conventional backups and are useful for taking backups for special purposes without disrupting the normal backup chain. This is particularly useful in AlwaysOn Availability Group scenarios.
Specifies a database object to backup.
Specifies a description for the backup set. This description is stored in the backup media and can be useful for identifying backups.
Specifies that the backup should be performed without any confirmation.
Specifies that the backup media should be initialized (overwritten) rather than appending to existing backup sets. Use with caution as this will destroy any existing backups on the media.
Returns the database object that was backed up. By default, this command does not generate any output.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the number of days that must elapse before the backup media can be overwritten. This provides protection against accidental overwrites.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Performs a differential backup of the database named MyDatabase .
Performs a transaction log backup of the database named MyDatabase .
Performs a copy-only full backup of the database named MyDatabase . This backup does not affect the normal backup chain.
Performs a compressed full backup with checksum verification of the database named MyDatabase .
Performs a full backup of the database named MyDatabase and returns the database object for further pipeline operations.

maml:description.maml:para: Backs up the encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Backs up the encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `BackupEncryptionKey` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

The encryption key is essential for decrypting stored credentials and connection strings in the report server database. This backup should be stored securely and is required for disaster recovery or migration scenarios.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the credentials to use when accessing a UNC path. Use this parameter when the Path is a network share that requires authentication.
Specifies the name of the temporary PSDrive to create when accessing a UNC path with credentials. Defaults to 'RSKeyBackup'. This parameter can only be used together with the Credential parameter.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after backing up the encryption key.
Specifies the password to protect the encryption key backup file. This password will be required when restoring the encryption key.
Specifies the full path where the encryption key backup file will be saved. The file extension should be .snk (Strong Name Key).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.

If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after backing up the encryption key.
Specifies the password to protect the encryption key backup file. This password will be required when restoring the encryption key.

{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Connect to a SQL Server Database Engine and return the server object.
This command connects to a SQL Server Database Engine instance and returns the Server object.

The credentials to use to impersonate a user when connecting to the SQL Server Database Engine instance. If this parameter is left out, then the current user will be used to connect to the SQL Server Database Engine instance using Windows Integrated authentication.
Specifies if encryption should be used.
String containing the SQL Server Database Engine instance to connect to. Default value is 'MSSQLSERVER'.

Specifies which type of logon credential should be used. The valid types are 'WindowsUser' or 'SqlLogin'. Default value is 'WindowsUser' If set to 'WindowsUser' then the it will impersonate using the Windows login specified in the parameter Credential. If set to 'SqlLogin' then it will impersonate using the native SQL login specified in the parameter Credential.
Specifies the TCP port number to use when connecting to the SQL Server instance. This parameter is only applicable when connecting via TCP/IP (Protocol = 'tcp'). Valid values are 1-65535.
If not specified for a named instance, the SQL Server Browser service will be used to determine the port. For default instances, port 1433 is used by default.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the network protocol to use when connecting to the SQL Server instance. Valid values are 'tcp' for TCP/IP, 'np' for Named Pipes, and 'lpc' for Shared Memory.
If not specified, the connection will use the default protocol order configured on the client.
String containing the host name of the SQL Server to connect to. Default value is the current computer name.
Set the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).

The credentials to use to impersonate a user when connecting to the SQL Server Database Engine instance. If this parameter is left out, then the current user will be used to connect to the SQL Server Database Engine instance using Windows Integrated authentication.
Specifies if encryption should be used.
String containing the SQL Server Database Engine instance to connect to. Default value is 'MSSQLSERVER'.

Specifies which type of logon credential should be used. The valid types are 'WindowsUser' or 'SqlLogin'. Default value is 'WindowsUser' If set to 'WindowsUser' then the it will impersonate using the Windows login specified in the parameter Credential. If set to 'SqlLogin' then it will impersonate using the native SQL login specified in the parameter Credential.
Specifies the TCP port number to use when connecting to the SQL Server instance. This parameter is only applicable when connecting via TCP/IP (Protocol = 'tcp'). Valid values are 1-65535.
If not specified for a named instance, the SQL Server Browser service will be used to determine the port. For default instances, port 1433 is used by default.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the network protocol to use when connecting to the SQL Server instance. Valid values are 'tcp' for TCP/IP, 'np' for Named Pipes, and 'lpc' for Shared Memory.
If not specified, the connection will use the default protocol order configured on the client.
String containing the host name of the SQL Server to connect to. Default value is the current computer name.
Set the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).

dev:type.maml:name: Returns the SQL Server server object.

maml:alert.maml:para: The protocol values ('tcp', 'np', 'lpc') are lowercase to match the SQL Server connection string prefix format, e.g., 'tcp:ServerName\Instance,Port'.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Connects to the default instance on the local server.
Connects to the instance 'MyInstance' on the local server.
Connects to the instance 'MyInstance' on the server 'sql.company.local'.
Connects to the default instance on the local server impersonating the Windows user 'DOMAIN\SqlUser'.
Connects to the default instance on the local server using the SQL login 'sa'.
Connects to the named instance 'MyInstance' on server '192.168.1.1' using TCP/IP on port 50200. The connection string format is 'tcp:192.168.1.1\MyInstance,50200'.
Connects to the default instance on server '192.168.1.1' using TCP/IP on port 1433. The connection string format is 'tcp:192.168.1.1,1433'.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables snapshot isolation for a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
This command disables snapshot isolation for a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance. Disabling snapshot isolation removes row-versioning and snapshot isolation settings for the database.
The command uses the SetSnapshotIsolation() method on the SMO Database object to disable row-versioning and snapshot isolation settings.

This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. maml:description.maml:para: Disables secure connection for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Disables secure connection (TLS/SSL) for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by setting the secure connection level to 0.

This command calls the `SetSecureConnectionLevel` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance with a level value of 0, which disables the secure connection requirement for connections to the Reporting Services web service and portal.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after disabling secure connection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Enables snapshot isolation for a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
This command enables snapshot isolation for a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance. Enabling snapshot isolation may require additional tempdb space and can affect transaction behavior.
The command uses the SetSnapshotIsolation() method on the SMO Database object to enable row-versioning and snapshot isolation settings to optimize concurrency and consistency.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables secure connection for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Enables secure connection (TLS/SSL) for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by setting the secure connection level to 1.

This command calls the `SetSecureConnectionLevel` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance with a level value of 1, which requires secure connections for all connections to the Reporting Services web service and portal.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after enabling secure connection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after enabling secure connection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

None. By default, this command does not generate any output.
When PassThru is specified, returns the MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting

Since SQL Server 2008 R2, the secure connection level is treated as an on/off toggle where any value greater than or equal to 1 enables secure connection.

maml:description.maml:para: Retrieves the current date and time from a SQL Server instance.

Retrieves the current date and time from a SQL Server instance using the specified T-SQL date/time function. This command helps eliminate clock-skew and timezone issues when coordinating time-sensitive operations between the client and SQL Server.
The command queries SQL Server using the server connection context, which does not require any database to be online.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies which T-SQL date/time function to use for retrieving the date and time. Valid values are: - `SYSDATETIME` (default): Returns datetime2(7) with server local time
- `SYSDATETIMEOFFSET`: Returns datetimeoffset(7) with server local time and timezone offset
- `SYSUTCDATETIME`: Returns datetime2(7) with UTC time
- `GETDATE`: Returns datetime with server local time
- `GETUTCDATE`: Returns datetime with UTC time
Specifies the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies which T-SQL date/time function to use for retrieving the date and time. Valid values are: - `SYSDATETIME` (default): Returns datetime2(7) with server local time
- `SYSDATETIMEOFFSET`: Returns datetimeoffset(7) with server local time and timezone offset
- `SYSUTCDATETIME`: Returns datetime2(7) with UTC time
- `GETDATE`: Returns datetime with server local time
- `GETUTCDATE`: Returns datetime with UTC time
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).

dev:type.maml:name: Returns the current date and time from the SQL Server instance.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Connects to the default instance and retrieves the current date and time using the default SYSDATETIME function.
Connects to the default instance and retrieves the current UTC date and time from the SQL Server instance.
Demonstrates using the server's clock for a point-in-time restore operation, avoiding clock skew issues between client and server.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the RsReportServer.config configuration file as an XML object.

Gets the RsReportServer.config configuration file for SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) or Power BI Report Server (PBIRS) as an XML document object. This allows programmatic access to configuration settings using standard XML navigation or XPath queries.

The configuration file path is automatically determined from the instance's setup configuration in the registry when using the `InstanceName` or `SetupConfiguration` parameter. Alternatively, a direct file path can be specified using the `Path` parameter.

Specifies the name of the Reporting Services instance. This is typically 'SSRS' for SQL Server Reporting Services or 'PBIRS' for Power BI Report Server. This parameter is mandatory when not passing a configuration object or a direct path.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the SQL Server Reporting Services configuration CIM instance.

Gets the SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server configuration CIM instance (`MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting`). This CIM instance can be used with other commands that manage Reporting Services configuration, such as `Enable-SqlDscRsSecureConnection` and `Disable-SqlDscRsSecureConnection`.

The returned CIM instance provides access to properties documented in \ MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting\ (https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/wmi-provider-library-reference/msreportserver-configurationsetting-properties), such as `SecureConnectionLevel`, `DatabaseServerName`, `VirtualDirectoryReportServer`, `WindowsServiceIdentityActual`, 'WindowsServiceIdentityConfigured' and methods for managing Reporting Services configuration.

By default, if the CIM instance is not found on the first attempt, the command will retry after a delay. This handles intermittent failures when the Report Server service or WMI provider is not immediately ready. Use `-SkipRetry` to disable retry behavior.
Specifies the name of the Reporting Services instance. This is a mandatory parameter.
Specifies the major version number of the Reporting Services instance. If not specified, the version is automatically detected using `Get-SqlDscRSSetupConfiguration`.
Specifies the number of retry attempts if the CIM instance is not found. Default is 1 retry attempt.
Specifies the delay in seconds between retry attempts. Default is 30 seconds.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
If specified, skips retry attempts and throws an error immediately if the CIM instance is not found.
Specifies the name of the Reporting Services instance. This is a mandatory parameter.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the number of retry attempts if the CIM instance is not found. Default is 1 retry attempt.

If specified, skips retry attempts and throws an error immediately if the CIM instance is not found.
Specifies the major version number of the Reporting Services instance. If not specified, the version is automatically detected using `Get-SqlDscRSSetupConfiguration`.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets execution log entries from the SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server database.

Gets execution log entries from the `ExecutionLog3` view in the report server database for SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) or Power BI Report Server (PBIRS). This view provides detailed records of report executions, including users, execution times, parameters, and rendering data.
The command automatically retrieves the database connection information (server name and database name) from the Reporting Services configuration CIM instance.
The `ExecutionLog3` view is available in SQL Server 2016 and later versions.
Specifies the name of the Reporting Services instance. This is typically 'SSRS' for SQL Server Reporting Services or 'PBIRS' for Power BI Report Server. This is a mandatory parameter.
Specifies the start time to filter execution log entries. Only entries with a TimeStart greater than or equal to this value will be returned.
Specifies the end time to filter execution log entries. Only entries with a TimeStart less than or equal to this value will be returned.
Specifies the user name to filter execution log entries. Supports SQL LIKE pattern matching (e.g., 'DOMAIN\%' or '%admin%').
Specifies the report path to filter execution log entries. Supports SQL LIKE pattern matching (e.g., '/Sales/%' or '%Revenue%').
Specifies the maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 1000. Set to 0 to return all rows (use with caution on large databases).
Specifies the credentials to use to impersonate a user when connecting to the report server database. If not provided, the current user's Windows credentials will be used.

Specifies which type of credentials are specified. The valid types are Integrated, WindowsUser, and SqlLogin. If WindowsUser or SqlLogin are specified then the Credential needs to be specified as well. Defaults to 'Integrated'.
Specifies the query statement timeout in seconds. Default is 600 seconds (10 minutes).
Specifies whether encryption should be used for the database connection.
Specifies that the query should be executed without any confirmation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the available IP addresses for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Gets the IP addresses available on the machine for use with SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `ListIPAddresses` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command returns information about IP addresses that can be used for URL reservations and SSL bindings. Each returned object includes the IP address, IP version (V4 or V6), and whether DHCP is enabled. If DHCP is enabled, the IP address is dynamic and should not be used for TLS bindings.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Returns an array of ReportServerIPAddress objects containing the IP
address, IP version, and DHCP status.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command calls the WMI method `ListIPAddresses`.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all available IP addresses for the Reporting Services instance.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the log file path for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server.

Gets the log file folder path for SQL Server Reporting Services (SSRS) or Power BI Report Server (PBIRS). The returned path is the ErrorDumpDirectory from the instance's setup configuration, which contains the LogFiles folder where service logs, portal logs, and memory dumps are stored.
The returned path can be used with `Get-ChildItem` and `Get-Content` to access and read the log files.
Common log file types in this folder: - ReportingServicesService*.log - Web service activity logs
- RSPortal*.log - Portal access and activity logs
- SQLDumpr*.mdmp - Memory dumps for error analysis

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the name of the Reporting Services instance. This is typically 'SSRS' for SQL Server Reporting Services or 'PBIRS' for Power BI Report Server. This parameter is mandatory when not passing a configuration object.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $logPath = Get-SqlDscRSLogPath -InstanceName 'SSRS' Get-ChildItem -Path $logPath -Filter 'ReportingServicesService*.log' | Sort-Object -Property LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 1 | Get-Content -Tail 100

maml:description.maml:para: Gets package information for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server.

Gets package information for a SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server executable file. The command returns file version information including product name, product version, file version, and other version-related metadata.

Specifies the path to the executable file to return version information for. The file must have a product name matching either 'Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services' or 'Microsoft Power BI Report Server'.
If specified, the ProductName validation is skipped. This allows retrieving version information for executables with different product names.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the path to the executable file to return version information for. The file must have a product name matching either 'Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services' or 'Microsoft Power BI Report Server'.
If specified, the ProductName validation is skipped. This allows retrieving version information for executables with different product names.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

This command returns the current service account name that is being used by the Reporting Services Windows service.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server setup configuration from the Registry.

Gets the SQL Server Reporting Services and Power BI Report Server setup configuration information from the Registry. This includes information like instance name, installation folder, service name, error dump directory, customer feedback settings, error reporting settings, virtual root, configuration file path, and various version information of the installed Reporting Services instance.
When no InstanceName is specified, it returns configuration information for all installed Reporting Services instances.
Specifies the instance name to return configuration information for. If not specified, configuration information for all Reporting Services and Power BI Report Server instances will be returned.

This command returns information about certificates that can be bound to the Reporting Services instance for SSL/TLS connections.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Gets the Report Server URLs for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by invoking the `GetReportServerUrls` CIM method on the `MSReportServer_Instance` CIM class. This returns the URLs for all configured Reporting Services applications (such as ReportServerWebService and ReportServerWebApp).
The setup configuration can be obtained using `Get-SqlDscRSSetupConfiguration` and passed via the pipeline.

The arrays are correlated by index, so Application[0] corresponds to UrlString[0].

See more information at: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/breaking-changes-in-sql-server-reporting-services-in-sql-server-2016

maml:description.maml:para: Returns the current permissions for a SQL Server login or server role.

Returns the current permissions for a SQL Server login or server role. The command can retrieve permissions for both user-defined and built-in server principals including SQL Server logins and server roles.
The command supports two modes of operation: 1. By name: Specify ServerObject, Name, and optionally PrincipalType 2. By object: Pass Login or ServerRole objects via pipeline
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are returned. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the name of the SQL Server login or server role for which the permissions are returned. This parameter is used in the default parameter set for backward compatibility.

Specifies the type(s) of principal to check. Valid values are 'Login' and 'Role'. If not specified, both login and role checks will be performed. If specified, only the specified type(s) will be checked. This parameter is used in the default parameter set for backward compatibility.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object. This parameter is used in the default parameter set for backward compatibility.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object. This parameter is used in the default parameter set for backward compatibility.

In such case the command will return `$null`. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error if the principal is missing.
The Login or ServerRole object must come from the same SQL Server instance where the permissions will be retrieved.

maml:description.maml:para: Returns TCP/IP address group information for a SQL Server instance.

Returns TCP/IP address group information for a SQL Server instance using SMO (SQL Server Management Objects). The command returns the IP address groups configured for the TCP/IP protocol, including their port configuration.
Each returned object contains an IPAddressProperties collection with properties such as 'TcpPort', 'TcpDynamicPorts', 'Enabled', and 'Active'.
Specifies the name of the SQL Server instance for which to return TCP/IP address group information.

maml:description.maml:para: Get SQL Server setup bootstrap log.

This command retrieves the SQL Server setup bootstrap log (Summary.txt) from the most recent setup operation. The log is typically located in the Setup Bootstrap\Log directory under the SQL Server installation path.
This command is useful for diagnosing SQL Server setup failures or understanding what occurred during the installation, upgrade, or rebuild operations.
Specifies the root SQL Server installation path to search for the setup log file. Defaults to 'C:\Program Files\Microsoft SQL Server'.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Grants server permissions to a principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

This command grants server permissions to an existing principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance. The principal can be specified as either a Login object (from Get-SqlDscLogin) or a ServerRole object (from Get-SqlDscRole).
Specifies that the permissions should be granted without any confirmation.
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are granted. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the permissions to be granted. Specify multiple permissions by providing an array of SqlServerPermission enum values.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the principal should also be granted the right to grant other principals the same permission.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: The Login or ServerRole object must come from the same SQL Server instance where the permissions will be granted. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'SilentlyContinue'` then the command will silently continue if any errors occur. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error on any failure.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Grants the specified permissions to the login 'MyLogin'.
Grants the specified permissions with grant option to the role 'MyRole' without prompting for confirmation.
Grants ConnectSql permission to all logins from the pipeline.

maml:description.maml:para: Imports a (preferred) module in a standardized way.
Imports a (preferred) module in a standardized way. If the parameter `Name` is not specified the command will imports the default module SqlServer if it exist, otherwise SQLPS.
If the environment variable `SMODefaultModuleName` is set to a module name that name will be used as the preferred module name instead of the default module 'SqlServer'.
The module is always imported globally.
Specifies the name of a preferred module.
Forces the removal of the previous module, to load the same or newer version fresh. This is meant to make sure the newest version is used, with the latest assemblies.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Forces the removal of the previous module, to load the same or newer version fresh. This is meant to make sure the newest version is used, with the latest assemblies.
Specifies the name of a preferred module.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Imports the default preferred module (SqlServer) if it exist, otherwise it will try to import the module SQLPS.
Will forcibly import the default preferred module if it exist, otherwise it will try to import the module SQLPS. Prior to importing it will remove an already loaded module.
Imports the specified preferred module OtherSqlModule if it exist, otherwise it will try to import the module SQLPS.

maml:description.maml:para: Initializes SQL Server Reporting Services.
Initializes SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `InitializeReportServer` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command initializes the report server with the current configuration settings. The report server must have a database connection configured before initialization.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after initialization.

Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Installs SQL Server Power BI Report Server.
Installs SQL Server Power BI Report Server using the provided setup executable.
Specifies the path where to find the SQL Server installation media. On this path the SQL Server setup executable must be found.
Specifies the product key to use for the installation, e.g. '12345-12345-12345-12345-12345'. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter Edition.
Specifies a free custom edition to use for the installation. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter ProductKey.
Specifies the file path where to write the log files, e.g. 'C:\Logs\Install.log'. By default log files are created under %TEMP%.
Specifies the folder where to install the product, e.g. 'C:\Program Files\Power BI Report Server'. By default the product is installed under the default installation folder.
Reporting Services: %ProgramFiles%\Microsoft SQL Server Reporting Services PI Report Server: %ProgramFiles%\Microsoft Power BI Report Server
Specifies how long to wait for the setup process to finish. Default value is `7200` seconds (2 hours). If the setup process does not finish before this time, an exception will be thrown.

Specifies that the acceptance of all license terms and notices for the specified features is required to be able to run unattended install. By specifying this parameter you acknowledge the acceptance of all license terms and notices for the specified features, the terms and notices that the setup executable normally asks for.
Specifies whether to upgrade the edition of the installed product. Requires that either the ProductKey or the Edition parameter is also assigned. By default no edition upgrade is performed.
Specifies whether the command will not ask for confirmation. Same as if Confirm:$false is used.
Specifies whether the command will return the setup process exit code.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies whether to suppress the restart of the computer after the installation is finished. By default the computer is restarted after the installation is finished.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Installs the database engine for the named instance MyInstance.
Installs the database engine for the named instance MyInstance and onboard the server to Azure Arc.
Installs the Azure Arc Agent on the server.
Installs SQL Server using the configuration file 'MySqlConfig.ini'.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a query on the specified database.
Executes a query on the specified database.
Specifies the credentials to use to impersonate a user when connecting. If this is not provided then the current user will be used to connect to the SQL Server Database Engine instance.
Specifies the name of the database to execute the T-SQL query in.
Specifies whether encryption should be used.
Specifies that the query should be executed without any confirmation.
Specifies the instance name on which to execute the T-SQL query.

Specifies which type of credentials are specified. The valid types are Integrated, WindowsUser, and SqlLogin. If WindowsUser or SqlLogin are specified then the Credential needs to be specified as well. Defaults to `Integrated`.
Specifies whether the command should return any result the query might return.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the query string to execute.
Specifies one or more text strings to redact from the query when verbose messages are written to the console. Strings will be escaped so they will not be interpreted as regular expressions (RegEx).
Specifies the server name where the instance exists.
Specifies the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Connects to the default instance and then runs a query to return all the database names in the instance.
Connects to the default instance and then runs the query to restore the database NorthWinds.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $serverObject = Connect-SqlDscDatabaseEngine Invoke-SqlDscQuery -ServerObject $serverObject -DatabaseName 'master' ` -Query "select * from MyTable where password = 'PlaceholderPa\ssw0rd1' and password = 'placeholder secret passphrase'" ` -RedactText @('PlaceholderPa\sSw0rd1','Placeholder Secret PassPhrase') ` -PassThru -Verbose

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows how to redact sensitive information in the query when the query string is output as verbose information when the parameter Verbose is used. For it to work the sensitiv information must be known and passed into the parameter RedactText. If any single character is wrong the sensitiv information will not be redacted. The redaction is case-insensitive.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: Invoke-SqlDscQuery -ServerName Server1 -InstanceName MSSQLSERVER -DatabaseName 'master' ` -Query 'SELECT name FROM sys.databases' -PassThru

dev:remarks.maml:para: Connects to the default instance and then runs a query to return all the database names in the instance.

maml:description.maml:para: Executes a scalar query on the specified server.

Executes a scalar query on the specified server using the server connection context. This command is designed for queries that return a single value, such as `SELECT @@VERSION` or `SELECT SYSDATETIME()`.
The command uses `Server.ConnectionContext.ExecuteScalar()` which is server-level and does not require any database to be online.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the scalar query string to execute.
Specifies the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).
Specifies one or more text strings to redact from the query when verbose messages are written to the console. Strings will be escaped so they will not be interpreted as regular expressions (RegEx).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the scalar query string to execute.
Specifies one or more text strings to redact from the query when verbose messages are written to the console. Strings will be escaped so they will not be interpreted as regular expressions (RegEx).
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the query StatementTimeout in seconds. Default 600 seconds (10 minutes).

dev:type.maml:name: Returns the scalar value returned by the query.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Connects to the default instance and then runs a query to return the SQL Server version.
Connects to the default instance and then runs the query to return the current date and time from the SQL Server instance.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $serverObject = Connect-SqlDscDatabaseEngine Invoke-SqlDscScalarQuery -ServerObject $serverObject -Query "SELECT name FROM sys.databases WHERE name = 'MyPassword123'" -RedactText @('MyPassword123') -Verbose

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows how to redact sensitive information in the query when the query string is output as verbose information when the parameter Verbose is used.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new SQL Agent Alert.
This command creates a new SQL Agent Alert on a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the name of the SQL Agent Alert to create.
Specifies the severity level for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 25. Cannot be used together with MessageId.
Specifies the message ID for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 2147483647. Cannot be used together with Severity.
Forces the action without prompting for confirmation.
If specified, the created alert object will be returned.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new SQL Agent Alert named 'MyAlert' with severity level 16.
Creates a new SQL Agent Alert named 'MyAlert' for message ID 50001 using pipeline input.
Creates a new SQL Agent Alert and returns the created object.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a server audit.
This command creates a server audit on a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

Specifies the filter that should be used on the audit. See \ predicate expression\ (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/create-server-audit-transact-sql)how to write the syntax for the filter.
Specifies the GUID found in the mirrored database. To support scenarios such as database mirroring an audit needs a specific GUID.
Specifies that the audit should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies the log location where the audit should write to. This can be SecurityLog or ApplicationLog.
Specifies the name of the server audit to be added.
Specifies what should happen when writing events to the store fails. This can be 'Continue', 'FailOperation', or 'Shutdown'.
If specified the created audit object will be returned.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the maximum delay before a event is written to the store. When set to low this could impact server performance. When set to high events could be missing when a server crashes.

Specifies that the ServerObject 's audits should be refreshed before creating the audit object. This is helpful when audits could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of audits it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
Specifies current server connection object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the filter that should be used on the audit. See \ predicate expression\ (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/create-server-audit-transact-sql)how to write the syntax for the filter.
Specifies the GUID found in the mirrored database. To support scenarios such as database mirroring an audit needs a specific GUID.
Specifies that the audit should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies the number of files on disk.
Specifies the maximum file size in units by parameter MaximumFileSizeUnit.
Specifies the unit that is used for the file size. This can be set to `Megabyte`, `Gigabyte`, or `Terabyte`.
Specifies the name of the server audit to be added.
Specifies what should happen when writing events to the store fails. This can be 'Continue', 'FailOperation', or 'Shutdown'.

Specifies the unit that is used for the file size. This can be set to `Megabyte`, `Gigabyte`, or `Terabyte`.
Specifies the amount of files on disk before SQL Server starts reusing the files. If not specified then it is set to unlimited.
Specifies the name of the server audit to be added.
Specifies what should happen when writing events to the store fails. This can be 'Continue', 'FailOperation', or 'Shutdown'.
If specified the created audit object will be returned.
Specifies the location where te log files wil be placed.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the maximum delay before a event is written to the store. When set to low this could impact server performance. When set to high events could be missing when a server crashes.

Specifies that the ServerObject 's audits should be refreshed before creating the audit object. This is helpful when audits could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of audits it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
This command creates a new database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance. It supports creating both regular databases and database snapshots.
Specifies whether Accelerated Database Recovery (ADR) is enabled for the database. This parameter requires SQL Server 2019 (version 15) or later.
Specifies whether new columns allow NULL by default unless explicitly specified (when ON).
Specifies whether comparisons to NULL follow ANSI SQL behavior (when ON, x = NULL yields UNKNOWN).
Specifies whether padding for variable-length columns (e.g., CHAR/VARCHAR) follows ANSI rules.
Specifies whether ANSI warnings are generated for certain conditions (when ON, e.g., divide by zero).
Specifies whether a query is terminated when an overflow or divide-by-zero error occurs.
Specifies whether the database closes after the last user exits.
Specifies whether creation of incremental statistics on partitioned tables is allowed.
Specifies whether single-column statistics are automatically created for query optimization.
Specifies whether the database automatically shrinks files when free space is detected.
Specifies whether statistics are updated asynchronously, allowing queries to proceed with old stats.
Specifies whether statistics are automatically updated when they are out-of-date.
Specifies whether Service Broker is enabled for the database.

Specifies the collation type for the system catalog. Valid values are DATABASE_DEFAULT and SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS. This property can only be set during database creation and cannot be modified afterward. This parameter requires SQL Server 2019 (version 15) or later.
Specifies whether automatic cleanup of change tracking information is enabled.
Specifies whether change tracking is enabled for the database.
Specifies the retention period value for change tracking information.
Specifies the units for the retention period (e.g., DAYS, HOURS).
Specifies whether open cursors are closed when a transaction is committed.
The name of the SQL collation to use for the new database. Default value is server collation.
The version of the SQL compatibility level to use for the new database. Default value is server version.
Specifies whether concatenation with NULL results in NULL (when ON).
Specifies the containment level of the database (NONE or PARTIAL).
Specifies whether ownership chaining across objects within the database is enabled.
Specifies whether SQL Server data retention policy is enabled at the database level. This parameter requires SQL Server 2017 (version 14) or later.
Specifies whether date correlation optimization is enabled to speed up temporal joins.
Specifies the LCID of the default full-text language.
Specifies the ID of the default language for the database.
Specifies the delayed durability setting for the database (DISABLED, ALLOWED, FORCED).
Specifies whether Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is enabled.

Specifies an array of DatabaseFileGroupSpec objects that define the file groups and data files for the database. Each DatabaseFileGroupSpec contains the file group name and an array of DatabaseFileSpec objects for the data files.
This parameter allows you to specify custom file and filegroup configurations before the database is created, avoiding the SMO limitation where DataFile objects require an existing database context.

For database snapshots, the FileName in each DatabaseFileSpec must point to sparse file locations. For regular databases, this allows full control over PRIMARY and secondary file group configurations.
Specifies the directory name used for FILESTREAM data.
Specifies the FILESTREAM access level for non-transactional access.
Specifies that the database should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies whether honoring Service Broker conversation priority is enabled.
Specifies whether full-text search is enabled.

Specifies whether to create a ledger database. Ledger databases provide tamper-evidence capabilities and are immutable once created. This parameter can only be set during database creation - ledger status cannot be changed after the database is created. This parameter requires SQL Server 2022 (version 16) or later, or Azure SQL Database.
Specifies whether forced parameterization is enabled for the database.
Specifies whether READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT isolation is ON.
Specifies whether the database is a SQL Data Warehouse database.
Specifies whether vardecimal compression is enabled.
Specifies the legacy cardinality estimator setting for the primary. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (for secondary replicas to use primary's setting).
Specifies the legacy cardinality estimator setting for secondary replicas. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (to use primary's setting).
Specifies whether cursors are local by default instead of global (when ON).
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for primary replicas.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for secondary replicas.
Specifies the maximum size of the database in bytes.
Specifies the mirroring partner server name (if configured).
Specifies the mirroring partner instance name (if configured).
Specifies the redo queue maximum size for mirroring/AGs.
Specifies the mirroring safety level (FULL/Off/HighPerformance).
Specifies the timeout in seconds for mirroring sessions.
Specifies the mirroring witness server (if used).
Specifies the name of the database to be created.
Specifies whether triggers are allowed to fire other triggers (nested triggers).
Specifies whether an error is raised on loss of precision due to rounding (when ON).
Specifies the name of the login that should be the owner of the database.
Specifies the page verification setting (NONE, TORN_PAGE_DETECTION, CHECKSUM).
Specifies the parameter sniffing setting for the primary. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (for secondary replicas to use primary's setting).
Specifies the parameter sniffing setting for secondary replicas. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (to use primary's setting).
Specifies the filegroup used for the Persistent Version Store (PVS). This parameter requires SQL Server 2019 (version 15) or later.
Specifies the path of the primary data files directory.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the query optimizer hotfixes setting for the primary. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (for secondary replicas to use primary's setting).
Specifies the query optimizer hotfixes setting for secondary replicas. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (to use primary's setting).
Specifies whether identifiers can be delimited by double quotes (when ON).
Specifies whether the database is in read-only mode.
The recovery model to be used for the new database. Default value is Full.
Specifies whether a trigger is allowed to fire itself recursively.

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before creating the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
Specifies the credential name for Stretch Database/remote data archive.
Specifies whether Stretch Database (remote data archive) is enabled.
Specifies the endpoint URL for remote data archive.
Specifies the linked server used by remote data archive.
Specifies whether to use federated service account for remote data archive.
Specifies the remote database name for remote data archive.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the target recovery time (seconds) for indirect checkpointing.
Specifies whether automatic cleanup of system-versioned temporal history is enabled. This parameter requires SQL Server 2017 (version 14) or later.
Specifies how full-text noise word behavior is controlled during queries.
Specifies whether implicit access to external resources by modules is allowed (use with caution).
Specifies the two-digit year cutoff used for date conversion.
Specifies the database user access mode. Valid values are Multiple, Restricted, and Single.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the name of the source database from which to create a snapshot. When this parameter is specified, a database snapshot will be created instead of a regular database. The snapshot name is specified in the Name parameter.

Specifies an array of DatabaseFileGroupSpec objects that define the file groups and data files for the database. Each DatabaseFileGroupSpec contains the file group name and an array of DatabaseFileSpec objects for the data files.
This parameter allows you to specify custom file and filegroup configurations before the database is created, avoiding the SMO limitation where DataFile objects require an existing database context.

For database snapshots, the FileName in each DatabaseFileSpec must point to sparse file locations. For regular databases, this allows full control over PRIMARY and secondary file group configurations.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new database named MyDatabase .
Creates a new database named MyDatabase with the specified collation and recovery model without prompting for confirmation.
Creates a database snapshot named MyDatabaseSnapshot from the source database MyDatabase without prompting for confirmation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $serverObject = Connect-SqlDscDatabaseEngine -InstanceName 'MyInstance' $primaryFile = New-SqlDscDataFile -Name 'MyDatabase_Primary' -FileName 'D:\SQLData\MyDatabase.mdf' -Size 102400 -Growth 10240 -GrowthType 'KB' -IsPrimaryFile -AsSpec $primaryFileGroup = New-SqlDscFileGroup -Name 'PRIMARY' -Files @($primaryFile) -IsDefault $true -AsSpec $secondaryFile = New-SqlDscDataFile -Name 'MyDatabase_Secondary' -FileName 'E:\SQLData\MyDatabase.ndf' -Size 204800 -AsSpec $secondaryFileGroup = New-SqlDscFileGroup -Name 'SECONDARY' -Files @($secondaryFile) -AsSpec $serverObject | New-SqlDscDatabase -Name 'MyDatabase' -FileGroup @($primaryFileGroup, $secondaryFileGroup) -Force

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new database named MyDatabase with custom PRIMARY and SECONDARY file groups using specification objects created with the -AsSpec parameter. All properties are set directly via parameters without prompting for confirmation.

Creates a new database named MyDatabase with database-scoped configuration settings: legacy cardinality estimation enabled, parameter sniffing disabled, and query optimizer hotfixes enabled, without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new database snapshot in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

This command creates a new database snapshot in a SQL Server Database Engine instance using SMO. It provides an automated and DSC-friendly approach to snapshot management by leveraging the existing `New-SqlDscDatabase` command for the actual creation.
Specifies the name of the source database from which to create a snapshot. This parameter is used in the ServerObject parameter set.

Specifies an array of DatabaseFileGroupSpec objects that define the file groups and data files for the database snapshot. Each DatabaseFileGroupSpec contains the file group name and an array of DatabaseFileSpec objects for the sparse files. When not specified, the cmdlet automatically generates DatabaseFileGroupSpec and DatabaseFileSpec entries based on the source database's file structure, resulting in sparse files being created in the default data directory with automatically generated names.
Specifies that the snapshot should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database snapshot to be created.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before creating the snapshot. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
Specifies the current server connection object. This parameter is used in the ServerObject parameter set.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the source database object to snapshot. This parameter can be provided via pipeline and is used in the DatabaseObject parameter set.

Specifies an array of DatabaseFileGroupSpec objects that define the file groups and data files for the database snapshot. Each DatabaseFileGroupSpec contains the file group name and an array of DatabaseFileSpec objects for the sparse files. When not specified, the cmdlet automatically generates DatabaseFileGroupSpec and DatabaseFileSpec entries based on the source database's file structure, resulting in sparse files being created in the default data directory with automatically generated names.
Specifies that the snapshot should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database snapshot to be created.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the name of the source database from which to create a snapshot. This parameter is used in the ServerObject parameter set.

Specifies an array of DatabaseFileGroupSpec objects that define the file groups and data files for the database snapshot. Each DatabaseFileGroupSpec contains the file group name and an array of DatabaseFileSpec objects for the sparse files. When not specified, the cmdlet automatically generates DatabaseFileGroupSpec and DatabaseFileSpec entries based on the source database's file structure, resulting in sparse files being created in the default data directory with automatically generated names.
Specifies that the snapshot should be created without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database snapshot to be created.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before creating the snapshot. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
Specifies the current server connection object. This parameter is used in the ServerObject parameter set.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: Specifies the SQL Server connection object to create the snapshot in.
Specifies the source database object to create a snapshot from.
Returns the newly created database snapshot object.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command is for snapshot creation only and does not support modification of existing snapshots.

Database snapshots are only supported in certain SQL Server editions (Enterprise, Developer, and Evaluation editions). The command will validate edition support before attempting to create the snapshot.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a new database snapshot named MyDatabase_Snapshot from the source database MyDatabase .
Creates a new database snapshot named MyDatabase_Snapshot from the database object MyDatabase without prompting for confirmation.
Creates a new database snapshot named MyDB_Snap from the source database MyDatabase without prompting for confirmation.
Creates a new database snapshot named MyDB_Snap from the source database MyDatabase with a specified sparse file location without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a new DataFile object for a SQL Server FileGroup and adds it to the FileGroup.

This command creates a new DataFile object and automatically adds it to the specified FileGroup's Files collection. The DataFile object represents a physical database file (.mdf, .ndf, or .ss for snapshots).

Returns a DatabaseFileSpec object instead of a SMO DataFile object. This specification object can be used with New-SqlDscFileGroup -AsSpec or passed directly to New-SqlDscDatabase to define data files before the database is created.
When this parameter is used, the command always returns a DatabaseFileSpec object, regardless of the PassThru parameter, and the FileGroup parameter is not available.

Specifies the physical path and filename for the DataFile. For database snapshots, this should point to a sparse file location (typically with an .ss extension). For regular databases, this should be the data file path (typically with .mdf or .ndf extension).

Specifies the amount by which the data file grows when it requires more space. The value is interpreted according to the GrowthType parameter (kilobytes or percentage). Only valid when used with the -AsSpec parameter to create a DatabaseFileSpec object.
Specifies the type of growth for the data file. Valid values are 'KB', 'MB', or 'Percent'. Only valid when used with the -AsSpec parameter to create a DatabaseFileSpec object.
Specifies that this data file is the primary file in the PRIMARY filegroup. Only valid when used with the -AsSpec parameter to create a DatabaseFileSpec object.
Specifies the maximum size to which the data file can grow, in kilobytes. Only valid when used with the -AsSpec parameter to create a DatabaseFileSpec object.
Specifies the logical name of the DataFile.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the initial size of the data file in kilobytes. Only valid when used with the -AsSpec parameter to create a DatabaseFileSpec object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies a DatabaseFileSpec object that defines the data file configuration including name, file path, size, growth, and other properties.
Specifies the FileGroup object to which this DataFile will belong. The DataFile will be automatically added to this FileGroup's Files collection.
Specifies that the DataFile object should be created without prompting for confirmation. By default, the command prompts for confirmation when the FileGroup parameter is provided.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a new encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Generates a new encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `ReencryptSecureInformation` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command generates a new symmetric encryption key and re-encrypts all stored secure information (such as credentials and connection strings) with the new key. This operation is typically performed when security requirements mandate key rotation.
WARNING: After generating a new encryption key, you should immediately back up the new key using `Backup-SqlDscRSEncryptionKey`. Any previous encryption key backups will no longer be valid.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after generating the new encryption key.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes a database from a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

Specifies that the role should be removed without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the server role to be removed.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's roles should be refreshed before trying to remove the role object. This is helpful when roles could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of roles it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough, or pass in RoleObject .
Specifies current server connection object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes encrypted information from SQL Server Reporting Services.

Removes all encrypted information stored in the SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server database by calling the `DeleteEncryptedInformation` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command deletes all encrypted data stored in the report server database, including stored credentials, connection strings, and other sensitive data.

WARNING: This is a destructive operation. After removing encrypted information, stored credentials and connection strings are permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. The report server will need to be re-initialized and data sources will need to be reconfigured with new credentials.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the encrypted information.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the encrypted information.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.

When PassThru is specified, returns the MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a destructive operation. Ensure you understand the impact before removing encrypted information. The Reporting Services service may need to be restarted after this operation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Removes all encrypted information from the Reporting Services instance.
Removes all encrypted information without confirmation.
Removes all encrypted information and returns the configuration CIM instance.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the encryption key from SQL Server Reporting Services.
Removes the encryption key from SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `DeleteEncryptionKey` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command deletes the current encryption key from the report server.

WARNING: This is a destructive operation. After removing the encryption key, stored credentials and connection strings cannot be decrypted. The report server will need to be re-initialized with a new or restored encryption key.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the encryption key.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the encryption key.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes an SSL certificate binding from SQL Server Reporting Services.

Removes an SSL certificate binding from SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `RemoveSSLCertificateBindings` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command removes an SSL certificate binding for a specific application, certificate, IP address, and port from the Reporting Services instance.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application from which to remove the SSL binding. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the thumbprint (hash) of the SSL certificate to unbind.
Specifies the IP address of the SSL binding to remove. Use '0.0.0.0' for all IP addresses. Default value is '0.0.0.0'.
Specifies the port number of the SSL binding to remove. Default value is 443.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the operation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the SSL certificate binding.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the thumbprint (hash) of the SSL certificate to unbind.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.

Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the operation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the SSL certificate binding.
Specifies the port number of the SSL binding to remove. Default value is 443.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

maml:description.maml:para: Removes the unattended execution account from SQL Server Reporting Services.

Removes the unattended execution account from SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `RemoveUnattendedExecutionAccount` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
After removing the unattended execution account, reports that require credentials but have no user context (such as scheduled subscriptions with data sources that require credentials) will fail.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the unattended execution account.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
