maml:description.maml:para: Removes a URL reservation for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Removes a URL reservation for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `RemoveURL` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command removes a URL reservation for a specific application in the Reporting Services instance. The application can be the Report Server Web Service, the Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+), or the Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application for which to remove the URL reservation. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the URL string to remove. The URL string format is typically 'http://+:80' or 'https://+:443' where the plus sign (+) is a wildcard that matches all hostnames.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the URL reservation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after removing the URL reservation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the URL reservation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).

maml:description.maml:para: Repairs an existing SQL Server Power BI Report Server installation.
Repairs an existing SQL Server Power BI Report Server installation using the provided setup executable.
Specifies the path where to find the SQL Server installation media. On this path the SQL Server setup executable must be found.
Specifies the product key to use for the repair, e.g. '12345-12345-12345-12345-12345'. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter Edition.
Specifies a free custom edition to use for the repair. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter ProductKey.
Specifies the file path where to write the log files, e.g. 'C:\Logs\Repair.log'. By default log files are created under %TEMP%.
Specifies the folder where to install the product, e.g. 'C:\Program Files\Power BI Report Server'. By default the product is installed under the default installation folder.
PI Report Server: %ProgramFiles%\Microsoft Power BI Report Server
Specifies how long to wait for the setup process to finish. Default value is `7200` seconds (2 hours). If the setup process does not finish before this time, an exception will be thrown.

Required parameter to be able to run unattended repair. By specifying this parameter you acknowledge the acceptance of all license terms and notices for the specified features, the terms and notices that the setup executable normally asks for.
Upgrades the edition of the installed product. Requires that either the ProductKey or the Edition parameter is also assigned. By default no edition upgrade is performed.
If specified the command will not ask for confirmation. Same as if Confirm:$false is used.
If specified the command will return the setup process exit code.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Suppresses the restart of the computer after the repair is finished. By default the computer is restarted after the repair is finished.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies a free custom edition to use for the repair. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter ProductKey.

If specified the command will not ask for confirmation. Same as if Confirm:$false is used.
Specifies the folder where to install the product, e.g. 'C:\Program Files\Power BI Report Server'. By default the product is installed under the default installation folder.
PI Report Server: %ProgramFiles%\Microsoft Power BI Report Server
Specifies the file path where to write the log files, e.g. 'C:\Logs\Repair.log'. By default log files are created under %TEMP%.

If specified the command will return the setup process exit code.
Specifies the product key to use for the repair, e.g. '12345-12345-12345-12345-12345'. This parameter is mutually exclusive with the parameter Edition.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies how long to wait for the setup process to finish. Default value is `7200` seconds (2 hours). If the setup process does not finish before this time, an exception will be thrown.

maml:alert.maml:para: The parameters are intentionally not described since it would take a lot of effort to keep them up to date. Instead there is a link that points to the SQL Server command line setup documentation which will stay relevant.

SQL Server Repair action does not accept the FEATURES parameter. Although Microsoft's documentation lists /FEATURES as required for the Repair action, the actual SQL Server setup executable (tested with SQL Server 2017 and SQL Server 2022) rejects this parameter with the error: "The setting 'FEATURES' is not allowed when the value of setting 'ACTION' is 'Repair'." SQL Server automatically repairs all installed features during a repair operation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Repairs all installed features of the instance 'MyInstance'.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates the T-SQL script to grant database permissions for Reporting Services.

Generates the T-SQL script to grant the necessary database permissions for a user account to access the report server database. This is done by calling the `GenerateDatabaseRightsScript` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
The generated script can be executed on a SQL Server Database Engine instance using `Invoke-SqlDscQuery` to grant the required permissions.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the name of the report server database to grant permissions on. This should match the database name used when creating the database.
Specifies the user account name to grant permissions to. This is typically the Reporting Services service account.
If specified, indicates that the database is on a remote server. By default, assumes the database is local.

When specified with Windows authentication (default), the UserName must be in the format `<domain>\<username>`. See the Microsoft documentation for more information about username requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/wmi-provider-library-reference/configurationsetting-method-generatedatabaserightsscript
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
If specified, indicates the user is a SQL Server authentication user. By default, assumes Windows authentication.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the name of the report server database to grant permissions on. This should match the database name used when creating the database.
If specified, indicates that the database is on a remote server. By default, assumes the database is local.

When specified with Windows authentication (default), the UserName must be in the format `<domain>\<username>`. See the Microsoft documentation for more information about username requirements: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/reporting-services/wmi-provider-library-reference/configurationsetting-method-generatedatabaserightsscript
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the user account name to grant permissions to. This is typically the Reporting Services service account.
If specified, indicates the user is a SQL Server authentication user. By default, assumes Windows authentication.

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.
Returns the T-SQL script as a string.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command should be run after creating the report server database using the script from `Request-SqlDscRSDatabaseScript`. After granting permissions, use `Set-SqlDscRSDatabaseConnection` to configure the Reporting Services instance to use the database.

To get the Reporting Services service account name, use the `WindowsServiceIdentityActual` property from the configuration object: `(Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration -InstanceName 'SSRS').WindowsServiceIdentityActual`

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates the T-SQL script to grant permissions on the 'ReportServer' database for the Reporting Services service account.
Gets the Reporting Services service account from the configuration object, generates the database rights script, and executes it on the RSDB SQL Server instance.
Generates the rights script for a remote database scenario.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates the T-SQL script to create a report server database.

Generates the T-SQL script to create a report server database for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `GenerateDatabaseCreationScript` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
The generated script can be executed on a SQL Server Database Engine instance using `Invoke-SqlDscQuery` to create the report server database.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

The Reporting Services service must be running before calling this command. For SQL Server Reporting Services the service name is `SQLServerReportingServices`, and for Power BI Report Server the service name is `PowerBIReportServer`. If the service is not running, a terminating error is thrown.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the name of the report server database to create. Common names are 'ReportServer' or 'ReportServer$InstanceName'.
Specifies the Language Code ID (LCID) to use for the database collation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the name of the report server database to create. Common names are 'ReportServer' or 'ReportServer$InstanceName'.
Specifies the Language Code ID (LCID) to use for the database collation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.
Returns the T-SQL script as a string.

maml:alert.maml:para: After creating the database, use `Request-SqlDscRSDatabaseRightsScript` to generate the script that grants the necessary permissions, then use `Set-SqlDscRSDatabaseConnection` to configure the Reporting Services instance to use the new database.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates the T-SQL script to create the 'ReportServer' database for the 'SSRS' Reporting Services instance.
Generates the database creation script and executes it on the RSDB SQL Server instance to create the report server database.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the database upgrade script for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Gets the database upgrade script for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `GenerateDatabaseUpgradeScript` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command generates a Transact-SQL script that can be used to upgrade the report server database schema to match the current Reporting Services version. This is useful during upgrade scenarios.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.
Returns the Transact-SQL script for upgrading the database.

maml:alert.maml:para: Review the generated script before executing it against a production database. Always back up the database before performing upgrades.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the database upgrade script for the Reporting Services instance.
Gets the upgrade script and executes it against the database.

maml:description.maml:para: Restarts a SQL Server Reporting Services instance.

Restarts a SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server Windows service. This command stops the service, optionally waits for a specified time, then starts the service again. It also restarts any dependent services that were running before the restart.
The command can be used in two ways: - With a configuration CIM instance from `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration`
- With a service name directly

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after restarting the service. Only applicable when using the Configuration parameter.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the number of seconds to wait after stopping the service before starting it again. This can be useful to allow the service to fully release resources before restarting. Default value is 0 seconds.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the name of the Reporting Services Windows service to restart. This parameter is used when the service name is known and a configuration object is not available.

Specifies the number of seconds to wait after stopping the service before starting it again. This can be useful to allow the service to fully release resources before restarting. Default value is 0 seconds.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after restarting the service. Only applicable when using the Configuration parameter.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the name of the Reporting Services Windows service to restart. This parameter is used when the service name is known and a configuration object is not available.

maml:description.maml:para: Restores a SQL Server database from a backup file.

This command restores a SQL Server database from a backup file using SQL Server Management Objects (SMO). It supports full, differential, transaction log, and file restores with options for file relocation, point-in-time recovery, and various recovery states.
Specifies the full path to the backup file. For full and differential restores, use the `.bak` extension. For transaction log restores, use the `.trn` extension.
Specifies the physical block size in bytes. Supported sizes are 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768, and 65536 bytes.
Specifies the number of I/O buffers to use for the restore operation.
Specifies that checksums should be verified during the restore operation.
Specifies a database object to restore to. This is used when restoring differential or log backups to an existing database.
Specifies the backup set number to restore when the backup file contains multiple backup sets.
Specifies that the restore should be performed without any confirmation.
Specifies that replication settings should be preserved during restore.
Specifies the maximum transfer size in bytes between SQL Server and the backup media.
Specifies that the database should be left in a restoring state after the restore operation. This allows additional differential or log backups to be restored. Cannot be used together with Standby.
Specifies that the restored database object should be returned.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies an array of RelocateFile objects that define how to relocate database files during restore. Each RelocateFile object contains a LogicalFileName and PhysicalFileName property.
Specifies that the existing database should be replaced. This is equivalent to the WITH REPLACE option in T-SQL.
Specifies the type of restore to perform. Valid values are 'Full', 'Differential', 'Log', and 'Files'. Default value is 'Full'.
Specifies that access to the restored database should be restricted to members of the db_owner, dbcreator, or sysadmin roles.

Specifies the path to the standby (undo) file. When specified, the database is left in standby mode, which allows read-only access while additional log backups can still be applied. Cannot be used together with NoRecovery.
Specifies the date/time after which to look for the mark specified by StopAtMarkName. If not specified, the restore stops at the first mark with the specified name.
Specifies the name of a marked transaction to stop at during restore. The restore includes the marked transaction.
Specifies the date/time after which to look for the mark specified by StopBeforeMarkName. If not specified, the restore stops before the first mark with the specified name.
Specifies the name of a marked transaction to stop before during restore. The restore does not include the marked transaction.
Specifies a point in time to restore the database to. This parameter is only valid for transaction log restores when the database uses the Full or Bulk-Logged recovery model.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Restores the encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Restores the encryption key for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `RestoreEncryptionKey` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command restores a previously backed up encryption key to the report server. This is required after migrating to a new server or in disaster recovery scenarios to decrypt stored credentials and connection strings in the report server database.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the credentials to use when accessing a UNC path. Use this parameter when the Path is a network share that requires authentication.
Specifies the name of the temporary PSDrive to create when accessing a UNC path with credentials. Defaults to 'RSKeyRestore'. This parameter can only be used together with the Credential parameter.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after restoring the encryption key.
Specifies the password that was used when backing up the encryption key.
Specifies the full path to the encryption key backup file (.snk).
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Brings a SQL Server database back online.

maml:description.maml:para: Removes (revokes) server permissions from a principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

This command removes (revokes) server permissions from an existing principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance. The principal can be specified as either a Login object (from Get-SqlDscLogin) or a ServerRole object (from Get-SqlDscRole).
Specifies that the permissions should be revoked without any confirmation.
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are revoked. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the permissions to revoke. Specify multiple permissions by providing an array of SqlServerPermission enum values.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies that the right to grant the permission should also be revoked, and the revocation will cascade to other principals that the grantee has granted the same permission to.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: The Login or ServerRole object must come from the same SQL Server instance where the permissions will be revoked. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'SilentlyContinue'` then the command will silently continue if any errors occur. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error on any failure.
When revoking permission with -WithGrant, both the grantee and all the other users the grantee has granted the same permission to, will also get their permission revoked.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Revokes the specified permissions from the login 'MyLogin'.
Revokes the specified permissions and the right to grant them from the role 'MyRole' with cascading effect, without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Downloads SQL Server media from a provided URL and saves the downloaded media file to the specified file path
The Save-SqlDscSqlServerMediaFile function downloads SQL Server media from a provided URL and saves the downloaded media file to the specified file path.
If the URL ends with ".exe", it is treated as an executable that downloads an ISO. If it doesn't, it is treated as a direct link to an ISO.
The function also prints the SHA1 hash of the downloaded file.
The URL of the SQL Server media to download.
The file path where the downloaded media file should be saved.
The file name of the downloaded media file. Defaults to media.iso if not provided.
The language parameter specifies the language of the downloaded iso. This parameter is only used when the provided URL is an executable file. Defaults to 'en-US' if not provided.
Forces the download of the media file even if the file already exists at the specified destination path.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Disables verbose progress output during the download process.
When specified, and the URL points to an executable (.exe), the function will download the executable file without executing it. The file will be saved using the provided FileName parameter.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Updates a server audit.
This command updates an existing server audit on a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

Specifies that the audit GUID can be changed by dropping and recreating the audit. This parameter is required when modifying the AuditGuid property because SQL Server does not allow direct modification of the audit GUID. When specified, the audit will be dropped and recreated with all existing properties plus the new GUID value. This is a destructive operation that requires explicit opt-in.

Specifies the filter that should be used on the audit. See \ predicate expression\ (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/create-server-audit-transact-sql)how to write the syntax for the filter.
Specifies the GUID found in the mirrored database. To support scenarios such as database mirroring an audit needs a specific GUID.
Specifies an audit object to update.
Specifies that the audit should be updated without any confirmation.
Specifies the maximum file size in units by parameter MaximumFileSizeUnit. Minimum allowed value is 2 (MB). It also allowed to set the value to 0 which mean unlimited file size.
Specifies the unit used for the file size. This can be Megabyte, Gigabyte, or Terabyte.
Specifies the amount of files on disk before SQL Server starts reusing the files. If not specified then it is set to unlimited.
Specifies what should happen when writing events to the store fails. This can be 'Continue', 'FailOperation', or 'Shutdown'.
If specified the changed audit object will be returned.
Specifies the location where the log files will be placed.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the maximum delay before a event is written to the store. When set to low this could impact server performance. When set to high events could be missing when a server crashes.

Specifies that the audit object should be refreshed before updating. This is helpful when audits could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of audits it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts a SQL Server server object via the pipeline.
Accepts a SQL Server audit object via the pipeline.
Returns the updated audit object when using the **PassThru** parameter.
No output when the **PassThru** parameter is not specified.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command has the confirm impact level set to high since an audit is unknown to be enable at the point when the command is issued.
See the SQL Server documentation for more information for the possible parameter values to pass to this command: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/sql/t-sql/statements/create-server-audit-transact-sql

dev:remarks.maml:para: Updates the file audit named MyFileAudit by setting the path to 'E:\auditFolder' and the queue delay to 1000.
Updates the application log audit named MyAppLogAudit by setting the queue delay to 1000.
Updates the file audit named MyFileAudit by setting the path to ''E:\auditFolder' and the queue delay to 1000, and returns the Audit object.
Changes the audit GUID by dropping and recreating the audit with the specified GUID.

maml:description.maml:para: Set server configuration option value.

This command sets the value of a SQL Server Database Engine configuration option using SQL Server Management Objects (SMO). The function validates that the option exists and that the provided value is within the option's minimum and maximum range.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the name of the configuration option to set.
Specifies the value to set for the configuration option.
Suppresses prompts and overrides restrictions that would normally prevent the command from completing. When specified, the command will not prompt for confirmation before making changes.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Suppresses prompts and overrides restrictions that would normally prevent the command from completing. When specified, the command will not prompt for confirmation before making changes.
Specifies the name of the configuration option to set.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the value to set for the configuration option.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: SQL Server Management Objects (SMO) Server object representing a SQL Server instance.

maml:alert.maml:para: This function supports ShouldProcess, allowing the use of -WhatIf and -Confirm parameters.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the "Agent XPs" configuration option to enabled (1).
Sets the "cost threshold for parallelism" configuration option to 50.
Shows what would happen if the "max degree of parallelism" option was set to 4, without actually making the change.
Sets the "cost threshold for parallelism" configuration option to 25 without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets default objects of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

This command sets default objects of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance. It can set the default filegroup, default FILESTREAM filegroup, and default Full-Text catalog using SMO methods.
Specifies a database object to modify.
Sets the default filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default FILESTREAM filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default Full-Text catalog for the database. The catalog must exist in the database.
Specifies that the database defaults should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Sets the default filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default FILESTREAM filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default Full-Text catalog for the database. The catalog must exist in the database.
Specifies that the database defaults should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database to be modified.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before modifying the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough, or pass in DatabaseObject .
Specifies current server connection object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Sets the default filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default FILESTREAM filegroup for the database. The filegroup must exist in the database.
Sets the default Full-Text catalog for the database. The catalog must exist in the database.
Specifies that the database defaults should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database to be modified.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: Server object accepted from the pipeline (ServerObject parameter set).
Database object accepted from the pipeline (DatabaseObject parameter set).

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the default filegroup of the database named MyDatabase to MyFileGroup .
Sets the default Full-Text catalog of the database named MyDatabase to MyCatalog without prompting for confirmation.
Sets multiple default objects for the database named MyDatabase .

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the owner of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
This command sets the owner of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
The owner must be a valid login on the SQL Server instance. The command uses the SetOwner() method on the SMO Database object to change the ownership.
Specifies the database object to modify (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).

Specifies whether to drop any existing database users mapped to the specified login before changing the owner. This is required if a non-dbo user account already exists for the login being set as the new owner.
Specifies that the database owner should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the login that should be the owner of the database.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies whether to drop any existing database users mapped to the specified login before changing the owner. This is required if a non-dbo user account already exists for the login being set as the new owner.
Specifies that the database owner should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies the name of the database to modify.
Specifies the name of the login that should be the owner of the database.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before trying to get the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
This parameter is only used when setting owner using ServerObject and Name parameters.

dev:type.maml:name: The database object to modify (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the owner of the database named MyDatabase to sa .
Sets the owner of the database named MyDatabase to sa , dropping any existing user account mapped to the sa login before changing the owner.
Sets the owner of the database using a database object without prompting for confirmation.
Sets the owner and returns the updated database object.

maml:description.maml:para: Set permission for a database principal.
Set permission for a database principal.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the database name.
Specifies the name of the database principal for which the permissions are set.
Specifies the state of the permission.
Specifies the permissions.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command excludes fixed roles like db_datareader by default, and will always throw a non-terminating error if a fixed role is specified as Name .

If specifying `-ErrorAction 'SilentlyContinue'` then the command will silently ignore if the database (parameter DatabaseName ) is not present or the database principal is not present. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error if the database or database principal is missing.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the permissions for the principal 'MyPrincipal'.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets properties of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.
This command sets properties of a database in a SQL Server Database Engine instance.

The command supports a comprehensive set of settable database properties including configuration settings, security properties, performance settings, and state information. Users can set one or multiple properties in a single command execution.
All properties correspond directly to Microsoft SQL Server Management Objects (SMO) Database class properties and support the same data types and values as the underlying SMO implementation.
Specifies whether Accelerated Database Recovery (ADR) is enabled for the database. This parameter requires SQL Server 2019 (version 15) or later.
Specifies whether new columns allow NULL by default unless explicitly specified (when ON).
Specifies whether comparisons to NULL follow ANSI SQL behavior (when ON, x = NULL yields UNKNOWN).
Specifies whether padding for variable-length columns (e.g., CHAR/VARCHAR) follows ANSI rules.
Specifies whether ANSI warnings are generated for certain conditions (when ON, e.g., divide by zero).
Specifies whether a query is terminated when an overflow or divide-by-zero error occurs.
Specifies whether the database closes after the last user exits.
Specifies whether creation of incremental statistics on partitioned tables is allowed.
Specifies whether single-column statistics are automatically created for query optimization.
Specifies whether the database automatically shrinks files when free space is detected.
Specifies whether statistics are updated asynchronously, allowing queries to proceed with old stats.
Specifies whether statistics are automatically updated when they are out-of-date.
Specifies whether Service Broker is enabled for the database.
Specifies whether automatic cleanup of change tracking information is enabled.
Specifies whether change tracking is enabled for the database.
Specifies the retention period value for change tracking information.
Specifies the units for the retention period (e.g., DAYS, HOURS).
Specifies whether open cursors are closed when a transaction is committed.
Specifies the default collation for the database.
Specifies the database compatibility level (affects query processor behavior and features).
Specifies whether concatenation with NULL results in NULL (when ON).
Specifies the containment level of the database (NONE or PARTIAL).
Specifies the database object to modify (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).
Specifies whether ownership chaining across objects within the database is enabled.
Specifies whether SQL Server data retention policy is enabled at the database level. This parameter requires SQL Server 2017 (version 14) or later.
Specifies whether date correlation optimization is enabled to speed up temporal joins.
Specifies the LCID of the default full-text language.
Specifies the ID of the default language for the database.
Specifies the delayed durability setting for the database (DISABLED, ALLOWED, FORCED).
Specifies whether Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is enabled.
Specifies the directory name used for FILESTREAM data.
Specifies the FILESTREAM access level for non-transactional access.
Specifies that the database should be modified without any confirmation.
Specifies whether honoring Service Broker conversation priority is enabled.
Specifies whether full-text search is enabled.
Specifies whether forced parameterization is enabled for the database.
Specifies whether READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT isolation is ON.
Specifies whether the database is a SQL Data Warehouse database.
Specifies whether vardecimal compression is enabled.
Specifies the legacy cardinality estimator setting for the primary. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (for secondary replicas to use primary's setting).
Specifies the legacy cardinality estimator setting for secondary replicas. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (to use primary's setting).
Specifies whether cursors are local by default instead of global (when ON).
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for primary replicas.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for secondary replicas.
Specifies the maximum size of the database in bytes.
Specifies the mirroring partner server name (if configured).
Specifies the mirroring partner instance name (if configured).
Specifies the redo queue maximum size for mirroring/AGs.
Specifies the mirroring safety level (FULL/Off/HighPerformance).
Specifies the timeout in seconds for mirroring sessions.
Specifies the mirroring witness server (if used).
Specifies whether triggers are allowed to fire other triggers (nested triggers).
Specifies whether an error is raised on loss of precision due to rounding (when ON).
Specifies the page verification setting (NONE, TORN_PAGE_DETECTION, CHECKSUM).
Specifies the parameter sniffing setting for the primary. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (for secondary replicas to use primary's setting).
Specifies the parameter sniffing setting for secondary replicas. Valid values are Off, On, or Primary (to use primary's setting).
Specifies that the database object should be returned after modification.
Specifies the filegroup used for the Persistent Version Store (PVS). This parameter requires SQL Server 2019 (version 15) or later.

maml:alert.maml:para: The following database properties are read-only after creation and cannot be modified using this command:

- CatalogCollation : The catalog-level collation used for metadata and temporary objects. This property is marked as ReadOnlyAfterCreation in the SMO Database class and can only be set during database creation (e.g., using `New-SqlDscDatabase` or CREATE DATABASE statements).

- DatabaseSnapshotBaseName : The base name of the source database for a database snapshot. This property is marked as ReadOnlyAfterCreation in the SMO Database class. To create database snapshots, use the `New-SqlDscDatabaseSnapshot` or `New-SqlDscDatabase` command with the `-DatabaseSnapshotBaseName` parameter.

- IsLedger : Specifies whether the database is a ledger database. Ledger status can only be set during database creation using the `New-SqlDscDatabase` command with the `-IsLedger` parameter. Once a database is created, its ledger status cannot be changed.
There are some database properties that require method calls instead of direct property assignment and will be supported through separate commands, e.g. `Set-SqlDscDatabaseDefaultFileGroup`.

Azure SQL Database service tier and service objective changes should be managed using Azure management cmdlets (e.g., `Set-AzSqlDatabase` with `-Edition` and `-RequestedServiceObjectiveName` parameters) rather than through SMO.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the recovery model of the database named MyDatabase to Simple .

Sets the compatibility level and trustworthy property of the database without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the report server database connection for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Sets the report server database connection for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `SetDatabaseConnection` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command configures which database the report server should use for storing report definitions, metadata, and other report server data.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the name of the server that hosts the report server database.
Specifies the name of the SQL Server instance that hosts the report server database. If not specified, the default instance is used.
Specifies the name of the report server database. Common names are 'ReportServer' or 'ReportServer$InstanceName'.
Specifies the type of credentials to use for the database connection. Valid values are: - 'Windows': Windows authentication with specified credentials.
- 'SqlServer': SQL Server authentication with specified credentials.
- 'ServiceAccount': Windows Service integrated security using the
report server service account.
The default is 'ServiceAccount'.
Specifies the credentials for connecting to the database when using 'Windows' or 'SqlServer' credentials type. This parameter is required when Type is 'Windows' or 'SqlServer'.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after setting the database connection.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the database timeout settings for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Sets the database logon timeout and/or query timeout for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `SetDatabaseLogonTimeout` and/or `SetDatabaseQueryTimeout` methods on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
At least one of `LogonTimeout` or `QueryTimeout` must be specified. Both can be specified together to set both values in a single call.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
Specifies the default timeout value, in seconds, for report server database connections. A value of 0 means no timeout.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after setting the timeout values.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the service account for SQL Server Reporting Services.

Sets the Windows service account for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `SetWindowsServiceIdentity` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.

This command changes the Windows service account that the Reporting Services service runs under. Sets file permissions on files and folders in the report server installation directory. The account requires LogonAsService rights in Windows, the specified account will be granted this right.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the credentials for the new service account. The username should be in the format 'DOMAIN\Username' for domain accounts or 'Username' for local accounts.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after setting the service account.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
If specified, restarts the Reporting Services service after changing the service account. The service must be restarted for the change to take effect.
If specified, suppresses the warning message about URL reservations needing to be updated when the service account changes.

Indicates that the account specified is a built-in Windows account such as 'NT AUTHORITY\NetworkService' or 'NT AUTHORITY\LocalSystem'. When this switch is used, only the username portion of the Credential is used and the password is ignored.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets SSL certificate bindings for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Sets the SSL certificate bindings for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by managing bindings through the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command replaces existing SSL certificate bindings for a specific application with the specified binding. Any existing bindings for the application that don't match will be removed.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application for which to set the SSL binding. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the thumbprint (hash) of the SSL certificate to bind. The certificate must be installed in the local machine certificate store.
Specifies the IP address for the SSL binding. Use '0.0.0.0' to bind to all IP addresses. Default value is '0.0.0.0'.
Specifies the port number for the SSL binding. Default value is 443.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the operation. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after setting the SSL certificate binding.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the unattended execution account for SQL Server Reporting Services.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the URL reservations for a SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server application to the specified list, or recreates all existing URL reservations.

The `Set-SqlDscRSUrlReservation` command can operate in two modes: Set Mode (default): Ensures that only the specified URL reservations exist for the given application. It removes any existing URL reservations that are not in the specified list and adds any URLs that are not currently reserved. Recreate Mode: When using the `-RecreateExisting` parameter, the command removes and re-adds all existing URL reservations for all applications. This is useful after changing the Windows service account, as URL reservations are tied to a specific service account and must be recreated to use the new account.
This command uses the `Get-SqlDscRSUrlReservation`, `Add-SqlDscRSUrlReservation`, and `Remove-SqlDscRSUrlReservation` commands internally.
Specifies the Reporting Services application for which to set URL reservations. Valid values are: ReportServerWebService, ReportServerWebApp, ReportManager. This parameter is only used in Set mode.
Specifies the Reporting Services configuration CIM instance. This is typically obtained by calling the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command.
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.

Specifies the locale identifier (LCID) for the URL reservation. If not specified, the operating system language code is used. Note that the LCID used when creating a URL reservation is not stored or retrievable, so when using `-RecreateExisting`, the LCID cannot be determined from the existing reservations and defaults to the OS language.
If specified, returns the Reporting Services configuration CIM instance.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies one or more URL strings to reserve. Any existing URL reservations for the application that are not in this list will be removed. This parameter is only used in Set mode.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

If specified, returns the Reporting Services configuration CIM instance.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

This is useful after changing the Windows service account, as URL reservations are tied to a specific service account and must be recreated to use the new account. This parameter cannot be used with `-Application` or `-UrlString`.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets the virtual directory for SQL Server Reporting Services.
Sets the virtual directory for SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server by calling the `SetVirtualDirectory` method on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
This command must be called before URL reservations can be added for a Reporting Services application. The virtual directory defines the path segment in the URL used to access the application.
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
Specifies the application for which to set the virtual directory. Valid values are: - 'ReportServerWebService': The Report Server Web Service.
- 'ReportServerWebApp': The Reports web application (SQL Server 2016+).
- 'ReportManager': The Report Manager (SQL Server 2014 and earlier).
Specifies the virtual directory name. This is the path segment used in the URL to access the application. Common values are 'ReportServer' for the web service and 'Reports' for the web portal.
Specifies the language code identifier (LCID) for the virtual directory. If not specified, defaults to the operating system language. Common values include 1033 for English (US).
If specified, suppresses the confirmation prompt.
If specified, returns the configuration CIM instance after setting the virtual directory.

maml:description.maml:para: Sets exact server permissions for a principal.

This command sets the exact server permissions for a principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance. The permissions passed in will be the only permissions set for the principal - any existing permissions not specified will be revoked.
The principal can be specified as either a Login object (from Get-SqlDscLogin) or a ServerRole object (from Get-SqlDscRole).

This command internally uses Get-SqlDscServerPermission, Grant-SqlDscServerPermission, Deny-SqlDscServerPermission, and Revoke-SqlDscServerPermission to ensure the principal has exactly the permissions specified.

Specifies the permissions that should be denied. The permissions specified will be the exact denied permissions - any existing denied permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing Deny permissions are left unchanged.
Specifies that the permissions should be set without any confirmation.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted. The permissions specified will be the exact granted permissions - any existing granted permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing Grant permissions are left unchanged.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted with the grant option. The permissions specified will be the exact grant-with-grant permissions - any existing grant-with-grant permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing GrantWithGrant permissions are left unchanged.
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are set. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies that the permissions should be set without any confirmation.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted. The permissions specified will be the exact granted permissions - any existing granted permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing Grant permissions are left unchanged.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted with the grant option. The permissions specified will be the exact grant-with-grant permissions - any existing grant-with-grant permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing GrantWithGrant permissions are left unchanged.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the ServerRole object for which the permissions are set. This parameter accepts pipeline input.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted. The permissions specified will be the exact granted permissions - any existing granted permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing Grant permissions are left unchanged.

Specifies the permissions that should be granted with the grant option. The permissions specified will be the exact grant-with-grant permissions - any existing grant-with-grant permissions not in this list will be revoked. If this parameter is omitted (not specified), existing GrantWithGrant permissions are left unchanged.
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are set. This parameter accepts pipeline input.

Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

maml:alert.maml:para: The Login or ServerRole object must come from the same SQL Server instance where the permissions will be set. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'SilentlyContinue'` then the command will silently continue if any errors occur. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error on any failure.

> [!IMPORTANT] > This command only modifies permission categories that are explicitly specified. > If you omit a parameter (e.g., don't specify `-Grant`), permissions in that > category are left unchanged. However, if you specify a parameter (even as an > empty array like `-Grant @()`), the command sets exact permissions for that > category only - revoking any permissions not in the list. This allows you to > independently manage Grant, GrantWithGrant, and Deny permissions without > affecting the other categories.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets the exact granted permissions for the login 'MyLogin'. Any other granted permissions will be revoked.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- EXAMPLE 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: $serverInstance = Connect-SqlDscDatabaseEngine $login = $serverInstance | Get-SqlDscLogin -Name 'MyLogin' Set-SqlDscServerPermission -Login $login -Grant ConnectSql -GrantWithGrant AlterAnyDatabase -Deny ViewAnyDatabase

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sets exact permissions for the login 'MyLogin': grants ConnectSql, grants AlterAnyDatabase with grant option, and denies ViewAnyDatabase. Any other permissions will be revoked.
Revokes all granted permissions from the role 'MyRole' without prompting for confirmation.

maml:description.maml:para: Starts the Reporting Services web service.

This command preserves the current state of the Windows service. If the web service is already enabled, the command proceeds without error (idempotent behavior).
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

maml:description.maml:para: Takes a SQL Server database offline.

This command takes a SQL Server database offline, making it temporarily unavailable. It is useful for maintenance scenarios, backups, or when you need to restrict access temporarily. The command uses the SMO Database.SetOffline() method to suspend the database.
Specifies the database object to take offline (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).

Specifies that the database should be forced offline even if users are connected. When specified, active connections will be disconnected immediately with rollback. Use this parameter with caution as it can disrupt active sessions.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after the operation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Specifies that the database should be forced offline even if users are connected. When specified, active connections will be disconnected immediately with rollback. Use this parameter with caution as it can disrupt active sessions.
Specifies the name of the database to take offline.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after the operation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before trying to get the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
This parameter is only used when suspending a database using ServerObject and Name parameters.
Specifies current server connection object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the database object to take offline (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).

Specifies that the database should be forced offline even if users are connected. When specified, active connections will be disconnected immediately with rollback. Use this parameter with caution as it can disrupt active sessions.
Specifies the name of the database to take offline.
Specifies that the database object should be returned after the operation.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before trying to get the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
This parameter is only used when suspending a database using ServerObject and Name parameters.
Specifies current server connection object.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

dev:type.maml:name: The database object to take offline (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).
By default, no output is returned.
When PassThru is specified, the updated database object is returned.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Takes the database named MyDatabase offline.
Takes the database offline using a database object, forcing disconnection of any active users.
Takes the database offline and returns the updated database object.
Takes the database offline using pipeline input, forcing disconnection of any active users.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if a SQL Agent Alert has the specified properties.
This command tests if a SQL Agent Alert on a SQL Server Database Engine instance has the specified properties. At least one property parameter must be specified.
Specifies the SQL Agent Alert object to test.
Specifies the expected message ID for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 2147483647. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this message ID.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the expected severity level for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 25. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this severity.
Specifies the expected message ID for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 2147483647. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this message ID.
Specifies the name of the SQL Agent Alert to test.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the expected severity level for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 25. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this severity.
Specifies the SQL Agent Alert object to test.
Specifies the expected message ID for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 2147483647. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this message ID.
Specifies the name of the SQL Agent Alert to test.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the expected severity level for the SQL Agent Alert. Valid range is 0 to 25. If specified, the command will return $true only if the alert exists and has this severity.

dev:type.maml:name: SQL Server Database Engine instance object.
Returns `$true` if the alert has the specified properties; otherwise `$false`.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Tests if the SQL Agent Alert named 'MyAlert' exists and has severity level 16.
Tests if the SQL Agent Alert named 'MyAlert' exists and has message ID 50001.
Tests if the SQL Agent Alert has severity level 16 using alert object pipeline input.

maml:description.maml:para: Verifies the integrity of a SQL Server backup file.

This command verifies the integrity of a SQL Server backup file using SQL Server Management Objects (SMO). It uses the Restore.SqlVerify() method to check that the backup file is readable and that all data can be successfully read.
Specifies the current server connection object.
Specifies the full path to the backup file to verify.
Specifies the backup set number to verify when the backup file contains multiple backup sets. If not specified, the first backup set is verified.
Specifies whether to load the backup history during verification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the full path to the backup file to verify.
Specifies the backup set number to verify when the backup file contains multiple backup sets. If not specified, the first backup set is verified.
Specifies whether to load the backup history during verification.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the current server connection object.

dev:type.maml:name: Server object accepted from the pipeline.
Returns $true if the backup file is valid, $false otherwise. Use
-Verbose to see detailed error messages when verification fails.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Verifies the integrity of the specified backup file and returns $true if the backup is valid, or $false if it is not.
Verifies the integrity of the specified backup file and loads the backup history during verification.

maml:description.maml:para: Test if server configuration option has the specified value.

This command tests whether a SQL Server Database Engine configuration option has the specified value using SQL Server Management Objects (SMO). The function validates that the option exists and compares the current value with the expected value.
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the name of the configuration option to test.
Specifies the expected value for the configuration option.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the name of the configuration option to test.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies current server connection object.
Specifies the expected value for the configuration option.

dev:type.maml:name: SQL Server Management Objects (SMO) Server object representing a SQL Server instance.
Returns $true if the configuration option has the specified value, $false otherwise.

maml:alert.maml:para: This function does not support ShouldProcess as it is a read-only operation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Tests if the "Agent XPs" configuration option is enabled (1).
Tests if the "cost threshold for parallelism" configuration option is set to 50.
Tests if the "max degree of parallelism" option is set to 4.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if database properties on a SQL Server Database Engine instance are in the desired state.
This command tests if database properties on a SQL Server Database Engine instance are in the desired state.

The command supports a comprehensive set of database properties including configuration settings, metadata, security properties, performance settings, and state information. Users can test one or multiple properties in a single command execution.
All properties correspond directly to Microsoft SQL Server Management Objects (SMO) Database class properties and support the same data types and values as the underlying SMO implementation.
Specifies whether Accelerated Database Recovery (ADR) is enabled for the database.
Specifies the number of active connections to the database (as observed by SMO).
Specifies whether the database participates in Active Directory integration features.
Specifies whether new columns allow NULL by default unless explicitly specified (when ON).
Specifies whether comparisons to NULL follow ANSI SQL behavior (when ON, x = NULL yields UNKNOWN).
Specifies whether padding for variable-length columns (e.g., CHAR/VARCHAR) follows ANSI rules.
Specifies whether ANSI warnings are generated for certain conditions (when ON, e.g., divide by zero).
Specifies whether a query is terminated when an overflow or divide-by-zero error occurs.
Specifies whether the database closes after the last user exits.
Specifies whether creation of incremental statistics on partitioned tables is allowed.
Specifies whether single-column statistics are automatically created for query optimization.
Specifies whether the database automatically shrinks files when free space is detected.
Specifies whether statistics are updated asynchronously, allowing queries to proceed with old stats.
Specifies whether statistics are automatically updated when they are out-of-date.
Specifies the synchronization state of the database in an Availability Group.
Specifies the name of the Availability Group to which the database belongs, if any.
Specifies the Azure SQL Database edition (e.g., Basic/Standard/Premium/GeneralPurpose/BusinessCritical).
Specifies the Azure SQL Database service objective (e.g., S3, P1, GP_Gen5_4).
Specifies whether Service Broker is enabled for the database.
Specifies whether the database collation is case-sensitive.
Specifies the catalog-level collation used for metadata and temporary objects.
Specifies whether automatic cleanup of change tracking information is enabled.
Specifies whether change tracking is enabled for the database.
Specifies the retention period value for change tracking information.
Specifies the units for the retention period (e.g., DAYS, HOURS).
Specifies whether open cursors are closed when a transaction is committed.
Specifies the default collation for the database.
Specifies the database compatibility level (affects query processor behavior and features).
Specifies whether concatenation with NULL results in NULL (when ON).
Specifies the containment level of the database (NONE or PARTIAL).
Specifies the date and time that the database was created.
Specifies the edition of the database engine hosting the database.
Specifies the engine type (e.g., Standalone, AzureSqlDatabase, SqlOnDemand).
Specifies the unique identifier (GUID) of the database.
Specifies the database object to test properties for (from Get-SqlDscDatabase).
Specifies whether ownership chaining across objects within the database is enabled.
Specifies whether SQL Server data retention policy is enabled at the database level.
Specifies whether date correlation optimization is enabled to speed up temporal joins.
Specifies the login that owns the database (dbo).
Specifies the name of the default filegroup for the database.
Specifies the name of the default FILESTREAM filegroup.
Specifies the default full-text catalog used for full-text indexes.
Specifies the LCID of the default full-text language.
Specifies the ID of the default language for the database.
Specifies the default schema name for users without an explicit default schema.
Specifies whether delayed transaction log flushes are enabled to improve throughput.
Specifies whether Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) is enabled.
Specifies the directory name used for FILESTREAM data.
Specifies the FILESTREAM access level for non-transactional access.
Specifies whether the database has a database encryption key (TDE).
Specifies whether the database has one or more files in Azure Storage.
Specifies whether the database contains memory-optimized (In-Memory OLTP) objects.
Specifies whether honoring Service Broker conversation priority is enabled.
Specifies the database ID (DB_ID). Unique numeric identifier assigned to the database by SQL Server.
Specifies the space used by indexes in KB.
Specifies whether the database is accessible to the current connection.
Specifies whether the database is a database snapshot.
Specifies whether the database is the source (base) of one or more snapshots.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_accessadmin for this database.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_backupoperator.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_datareader.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_datawriter.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_ddladmin.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_denydatareader.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_denydatawriter.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_manager (Azure role).
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_owner.
Specifies whether the caller is member of db_securityadmin.
Specifies whether the database is a Microsoft Fabric SQL database.
Specifies whether full-text search is enabled.
Specifies whether the database is enabled for SQL Ledger features.
Specifies whether the caller is member of the login manager role (Azure).
Specifies whether Database Mail host features are configured on this database.
Specifies whether the database is configured as the Management Data Warehouse.
Specifies whether MaxSizeInBytes is enforced for the database.
Specifies whether database mirroring is configured.
Specifies whether parameterization of queries is forced by default (when ON).
Specifies whether READ_COMMITTED_SNAPSHOT isolation is ON.
Specifies whether the database is an Azure Synapse (SQL DW) database.
Specifies whether the edition corresponds to Azure Synapse (DW).
Specifies whether the database is a system database (master, model, msdb, tempdb).
Specifies whether vardecimal storage format is enabled.
Specifies whether vardecimal storage format is supported by the server.
Specifies the timestamp of the last full database backup.
Specifies the timestamp of the last differential backup.
Specifies the timestamp when DBCC CHECKDB last completed successfully.
Specifies the timestamp of the last transaction log backup.
Specifies whether the legacy cardinality estimator is enabled for the primary.
Specifies whether the legacy cardinality estimator is enabled for secondary replicas.
Specifies whether cursors are local by default instead of global (when ON).
Specifies the reason why the transaction log cannot be reused.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for primary replicas.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for secondary replicas.
Specifies the maximum size of the database in bytes.
Specifies the memory allocated to memory-optimized objects (KB).
Specifies the memory used by memory-optimized objects (KB).
Specifies the mirroring failover LSN (if mirroring configured).
Specifies the unique mirroring ID for the database.
Specifies the mirroring partner server name (if configured).
Specifies the mirroring partner instance name (if configured).
Specifies the redo queue maximum size for mirroring/AGs.
Specifies the sequence number for mirroring role transitions.
Specifies the mirroring safety level (FULL/Off/HighPerformance).
Specifies the sequence for mirroring safety changes.
Specifies the current mirroring state of the database.
Specifies the timeout in seconds for mirroring sessions.
Specifies the mirroring witness server (if used).
Specifies the status of the mirroring witness.
Specifies whether triggers are allowed to fire other triggers (nested triggers).
Specifies whether an error is raised on loss of precision due to rounding (when ON).
Specifies the owner (login) of the database.
Specifies the page verification setting (NONE, TORN_PAGE_DETECTION, CHECKSUM).
Specifies whether parameter sniffing behavior is enabled on the primary.
Specifies whether parameter sniffing is enabled on secondary replicas.
Specifies the filegroup used for the Persistent Version Store (PVS).
Specifies the size of the Persistent Version Store in KB.
Specifies the path of the primary data files directory.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies whether query optimizer hotfixes are enabled on the primary.
Specifies whether query optimizer hotfixes are enabled on secondary replicas.
Specifies whether identifiers can be delimited by double quotes (when ON).
Specifies whether the database is in read-only mode.
Specifies the GUID for the current recovery fork of the database.
Specifies the database recovery model (FULL, BULK_LOGGED, SIMPLE).
Specifies whether a trigger is allowed to fire itself recursively.
Specifies the credential name for Stretch Database/remote data archive.

Specifies the endpoint URL for remote data archive.
Specifies the linked server used by remote data archive.
Specifies whether to use federated service account for remote data archive.

Specifies the timestamp of the last differential backup.
Specifies the timestamp when DBCC CHECKDB last completed successfully.
Specifies the timestamp of the last transaction log backup.
Specifies whether the legacy cardinality estimator is enabled for the primary.
Specifies whether the legacy cardinality estimator is enabled for secondary replicas.
Specifies whether cursors are local by default instead of global (when ON).
Specifies the reason why the transaction log cannot be reused.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for primary replicas.
Specifies the MAXDOP database-scoped configuration for secondary replicas.

Specifies the status of the mirroring witness.
Specifies the name of the database to test properties for. The logical database name as it appears in SQL Server.
Specifies whether triggers are allowed to fire other triggers (nested triggers).
Specifies whether an error is raised on loss of precision due to rounding (when ON).
Specifies the owner (login) of the database.
Specifies the page verification setting (NONE, TORN_PAGE_DETECTION, CHECKSUM).
Specifies whether parameter sniffing behavior is enabled on the primary.
Specifies whether parameter sniffing is enabled on secondary replicas.

Specifies whether query optimizer hotfixes are enabled on the primary.
Specifies whether query optimizer hotfixes are enabled on secondary replicas.
Specifies whether identifiers can be delimited by double quotes (when ON).
Specifies whether the database is in read-only mode.
Specifies the GUID for the current recovery fork of the database.
Specifies the database recovery model (FULL, BULK_LOGGED, SIMPLE).
Specifies whether a trigger is allowed to fire itself recursively.

Specifies that the ServerObject 's databases should be refreshed before trying to get the database object. This is helpful when databases could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on instances with a large amount of databases it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough.
This parameter is only used when testing properties using ServerObject and Name parameters.

Specifies the remote database name for remote data archive.
Specifies the replication options that are enabled for the database.
