Specifies the unique Service Broker identifier for the database.
Specifies the approximate size of the database in MB (as reported by SMO).
Specifies whether SNAPSHOT isolation is OFF/ON/IN_TRANSITION.
Specifies the free space available in the database (KB).
Specifies the general state of the SMO object.
Specifies the operational status of the database as reported by SMO.
Specifies the target recovery time (seconds) for indirect checkpointing.
Specifies whether automatic cleanup of system-versioned temporal history is enabled.
Specifies how full-text noise word behavior is controlled during queries.
Specifies whether implicit access to external resources by modules is allowed (use with caution).
Specifies the two-digit year cutoff used for date conversion.
Specifies the database user access mode (Multiple, Restricted, Single).
Specifies the user name for the current connection context (as seen by SMO).
Specifies the internal version number of the database.
Specifies whether a warning is emitted when objects are renamed.

Specifies that the database's principal collections (Users, Roles, and ApplicationRoles) should be refreshed before testing if the principal exists. This is helpful when principals could have been modified outside of the ServerObject , for example through T-SQL. But on databases with a large amount of principals it might be better to make sure the ServerObject is recent enough. When exclude parameters are specified (e.g., ExcludeUsers , ExcludeRoles , ExcludeApplicationRoles ), only the collections that will be used are refreshed to improve performance.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if SQL Server Reporting Services sites are accessible.
Tests if SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server web sites are accessible by making HTTP requests to the configured URLs.
This command can be used to verify that a Reporting Services instance is fully configured and responding to web requests after initialization.

The command supports two modes: - Configuration mode: Uses a CIM configuration instance to automatically detect the configured URLs from URL reservations. - URI mode: Uses explicitly specified URIs for the ReportServer and/or Reports sites.

When using the Configuration parameter set, a dynamic `-Site` parameter becomes available that allows selecting which sites to test. The available sites are determined from the URL reservations configured for the instance.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
When specified, returns a detailed object containing accessibility status, HTTP status codes, and URIs for each site instead of a simple boolean.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the interval in seconds between retry attempts. Defaults to 5 seconds.
Specifies the server name to use when constructing URLs from the configuration. Defaults to `$env:COMPUTERNAME`.
Specifies the maximum time in seconds to wait for the sites to become accessible. Defaults to 120 seconds.
When specified, returns a detailed object containing accessibility status, HTTP status codes, and URIs for each site instead of a simple boolean.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the explicit URI for the ReportServer web service to test. This parameter is used in the Uri parameter set.
Specifies the explicit URI for the Reports web portal to test. This parameter is used in the Uri parameter set.
Specifies the interval in seconds between retry attempts. Defaults to 5 seconds.
Specifies the maximum time in seconds to wait for the sites to become accessible. Defaults to 120 seconds.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
When specified, returns a detailed object containing accessibility status, HTTP status codes, and URIs for each site instead of a simple boolean.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the explicit URI for the ReportServer web service to test. This parameter is used in the Uri parameter set.
Specifies the explicit URI for the Reports web portal to test. This parameter is used in the Uri parameter set.
Specifies the interval in seconds between retry attempts. Defaults to 5 seconds.
Specifies the server name to use when constructing URLs from the configuration. Defaults to `$env:COMPUTERNAME`.
Specifies the maximum time in seconds to wait for the sites to become accessible. Defaults to 120 seconds.

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.
Returns `$true` if all specified sites return HTTP 200, `$false` otherwise.
When `-Detailed` is specified, returns an array of objects, one for
- `Accessible`: Boolean indicating if the site is accessible.
- `StatusCode`: HTTP status code returned from the site.

maml:alert.maml:para: This command uses `Invoke-WebRequest` with `-UseDefaultCredentials` to authenticate to the Reporting Services sites.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Tests if all configured sites for the SSRS instance are accessible. Returns `$true` if all sites return HTTP 200, `$false` otherwise.
Tests if only the ReportServerWebService site is accessible for the SSRS instance.
Tests if the specified ReportServer and Reports URIs are accessible using explicit URIs.
Tests if all configured sites are accessible and returns a detailed object with status information for each site.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if SQL Server Reporting Services is initialized.
Tests if SQL Server Reporting Services or Power BI Report Server is initialized by checking the `IsInitialized` property on the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance.
A Reporting Services instance is considered initialized when: - The report server database is configured
- The encryption keys are set up
- The service is ready to serve reports
The configuration CIM instance can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command and passed via the pipeline.

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies the `MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting` CIM instance for the Reporting Services instance. This can be obtained using the `Get-SqlDscRSConfiguration` command. This parameter accepts pipeline input.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:type.maml:name: Accepts MSReportServer_ConfigurationSetting CIM instance via pipeline.
Returns $true if the Reporting Services instance is initialized,

maml:alert.maml:para: This is a convenience wrapper around checking the `IsInitialized` property of the configuration CIM instance.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Returns $true if the Reporting Services instance 'SSRS' is initialized.

maml:description.maml:para: Tests if server permissions for a principal are in the desired state.

This command tests if server permissions for an existing principal on a SQL Server Database Engine instance are in the desired state. The principal can be specified as either a Login object (from Get-SqlDscLogin) or a ServerRole object (from Get-SqlDscRole).
Specifies that the test should verify if the permissions are denied to the principal.

Specifies that the test should verify that only the specified permissions are present and no additional permissions exist in the specified state. When this parameter is not used, the test will return true if the specified permissions are present, regardless of any additional permissions.
Specifies the Login object for which the permissions are tested. This parameter accepts pipeline input.

Specifies the desired permissions. Specify multiple permissions by providing an array of SqlServerPermission enum values that should be present in the specified state. An empty collection can be specified to test that no permissions are set for the principal.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

Specifies that the principal should have the right to grant other principals the same permission. This parameter is only valid when parameter Grant is used. When this parameter is used, the effective state tested will be 'GrantWithGrant'.

maml:alert.maml:para: The Login or ServerRole object must come from the same SQL Server instance where the permissions will be tested. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'SilentlyContinue'` then the command will silently continue if any errors occur. If specifying `-ErrorAction 'Stop'` the command will throw an error on any failure.

maml:description.maml:para: Uninstalls SQL Server Power BI Report Server.
Uninstalls SQL Server Power BI Report Server using the provided setup executable.
Specifies the path where to find the SQL Server installation media. On this path the SQL Server setup executable must be found.
Specifies the file path where to write the log files, e.g. 'C:\Logs\Uninstall.log'. By default log files are created under %TEMP%.
Specifies how long to wait for the setup process to finish. Default value is `7200` seconds (2 hours). If the setup process does not finish before this time, an exception will be thrown.
If specified the command will not ask for confirmation. Same as if Confirm:$false is used.
If specified the command will return the setup process exit code.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Suppresses the restart of the computer after the uninstallation is finished. By default the computer is restarted after the uninstallation is finished.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
If specified the command will not ask for confirmation. Same as if Confirm:$false is used.

maml:description.maml:para: Upgrades a SQL Server instance to a newer version.
Upgrades a SQL Server instance to a newer version using the Microsoft SQL Server setup executable. This command performs an in-place upgrade of an existing SQL Server instance.
See the link in the commands help for information on each parameter. The link points to SQL Server command line setup documentation.
Specifies the path where to find the SQL Server installation media. On this path the SQL Server setup executable must be found.
See the notes section for more information.
See the notes section for more information.
See the notes section for more information.
Specifies how long to wait for the setup process to finish. Default value is `7200` seconds (2 hours). If the setup process does not finish before this time, an exception will be thrown.

maml:description.maml:para: Upgrades a SQL Server instance to a different edition.

Upgrades a SQL Server instance to a different edition using the Microsoft SQL Server setup executable. This command performs an edition upgrade (for example, from Evaluation to Standard or Enterprise edition).

Specifies the path where to find the SQL Server installation media. On this path the SQL Server setup executable must be found.
